title: machaeranthera canescens subsp. viscosa
intro: Machaeranthera canescens subsp. viscosa, also known as sticky greasewood, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial and fungal infections, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Machaeranthera canescens subsp. viscosa can be made into teas, salves, or tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
